response: {"purpose":"Figure 3e compares the fraction of usable (non-PCR-duplicated) reads among uniquely mapped reads across three CLIP-seq methodologies: eCLIP, iCLIP (both ENCODE consortium and published datasets), and other published CLIP variants (PAR-CLIP and HITS-CLIP). By displaying individual data points overlaid with kernel density-smoothed histograms, the panel allows direct visual comparison of library complexity and PCR redundancy across a large number of experiments. This comparison matters because high PCR duplication reduces the effective information content of sequencing data and can inflate apparent binding signals, so demonstrating that eCLIP yields a higher fraction of usable reads than existing methods supports its superiority as a scalable, high-quality approach for RNA-binding protein target identification.","title":"PCR Duplication Rates Compared Across eCLIP, iCLIP, and CLIP Methods"}
